channel.title: Just In
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026, Australian Broadcasting Corporation. All Rights Reserved.

item.title: $250 tax break for millions in 'rebalance' of tax system for workers
item.description: Labor will raise taxes on investment properties and some trust funds, giving the revenue back to workers with a new $250 tax break under reforms it says will "rebalance" the Australian tax system.
item.dc:creator: Clare Armstrong

media:group.media:description: Treasurer Jim Chalmers and Finance Minister Katy Gallagher say the budget aims to tackle intergenerational inequity.

item.title: Chalmers jumped into the political no-go zone for one clear reason
item.description: One of the more striking elements from the now well entrenched program of pre-budget leaks is the lack of outrage from the population or even the opposition.
item.dc:creator: Ian Verrender

media:group.media:description: Treasurer Jim Chalmers said in his budget speech that the government had chosen "the hard road of reform, not the path of least resistance".

item.title: CGT changes could see investment shift to shares, budget suggests
item.description: Investors in stocks will be affected by changes to capital gains tax, but experts say the budget is still likely to see some investment move away from property and into shares.
item.dc:creator: Stephanie Chalmers, Jasper Wells, and David Taylor

media:group.media:description: Several tax measures to help small business with cash flow have been made permanent in the budget.

item.title: Australia's last baby boomer prime minister becomes King of the Kids
item.description: Tonight Anthony Albanese becomes the first serving prime minister in decades to take a hammer to the most sacred shibboleth in Australian domestic politics.
item.dc:creator: Annabel Crabb

media:group.media:description: Millennial and generation Z voters now constitute a majority of the electorate.

item.title: Negative gearing cut off for existing homes bought after tonight
item.description: Tax perks that have been used by property investors for decades to maximise profits and offset their losses will be restricted from tonight, in one of the most significant changes to the tax system in years.
item.dc:creator: Jake Evans

media:group.media:description: Decades-old tax perks for property investors are being restricted, and loopholes allowing wealthy families to pay less tax are being closed.

item.title: Your five-minute guide to this year's cost-of-living budget measures
item.description: Fresh tax cuts, an instant $1,000 tax reduction and cheaper medicines: here's what the federal government's latest budget means for Australians.
item.dc:creator: Hanan Dervisevic

media:group.media:description: The federal budget will target cost of living in what Treasurer Jim Chalmers says will "take pressure off Australians".

item.title: Find out if you're among the budget's winners or losers
item.description: In the first full budget since Labor's landslide election victory last year, the government is putting some money back in workers' pockets and increasing taxes on investors.
item.dc:creator: Holly Tregenza and Samantha Dick

item.title: Father allegedly high on 'nangs' in crash that killed daughters, as scuffle erupts outside court
item.description: A Perth father was allegedly high on 'nangs' during a crash that killed his two daughters, as a scuffle with media erupts at court.
item.dc:creator: Mya Kordic

media:group.media:description: The man is facing Midland Magistrates Court afte being charged with causing the girls' death.

item.title: The name of this health condition was 'very inaccurate'. Now it's changed
item.description: Polycystic ovary syndrome (PCOS) has been renamed polyendocrine metabolic ovarian syndrome (PMOS), a move health experts say will lead to faster diagnoses and better treatment for women of all ages.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Roberts and Anna Chisholm

media:group.media:description: About one in eight women have the complex lifelong condition.

item.title: LeBron contemplates potential retirement after NBA playoff exit
item.description: LeBron James says he has no idea what the future holds after being bounced out of the second round of the NBA playoffs.

media:group.media:description: LeBron James is still undecided on whether he will continue his NBA career into a 24th season after being eliminated from the play-offs.

item.title: Duo swindled $6 million from five victims in investment scam, court hears
item.description: Two men fleeced millions from five victims in a fraudulent property investment scheme, which one victim said had stolen half her superannuation and "ruined" her life.
item.dc:creator: Eva Blandis

media:group.media:description: Andre Langos and Sam Smith will be sentenced in South Australia's District Court next month.

item.title: Rankine says he has 'come a long way' since homophobic slur ban
item.description: After being forced to sit out of last year's finals series thanks to a suspension for a homophobic slur, Izak Rankine is confident he has grown.

media:group.media:description: Izak Rankine is relishing being back in the spotlight.

item.title: Property owner says he helped wounded officer after Dezi Freeman shot at police
item.description: A Porepunkah property owner says he helped a wounded police officer after the fatal shooting when Dezi Freeman killed two Victoria police officers.
item.dc:creator: Ashlee Aldridge

media:group.media:description: The owner of the property where two police officers were shot dead speaks out in new video.

item.title: Pakistan market blast kills at least nine people
item.description: A blast in a market kills at least nine people as Islamabad blames Kabul for a weekend attack that has left 15 police officers dead.

media:group.media:description: Police officers and local residents inspect the damage at the site of a bomb explosion.

item.title: Jacinta Nampijinpa Price leads emotional tributes for Kumanjayi Little Baby
item.description: Senator Jacinta Nampijinpa Price weeps through an impassioned tribute to her niece, Kumanjayi Little Baby, in federal parliament, amid a push to broaden the focus of an inquiry announced in the wake of her alleged murder.
item.dc:creator: Jacqueline Breen

media:group.media:description: Senator Jacinta Nampijinpa Price moved the motion and made an emotional speech in parliament on Tuesday.

item.title: Skipper keen to refloat yacht beached at Byron Bay and avoid $20K fine
item.description: The clock is ticking for a skipper scrambling to avoid a hefty fine by refloating a yacht beached at Byron Bay.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Ross

media:group.media:description: Jeff Sutton is looking at ways to refloat Wyuna 1.

item.title: Man charged with son's murder offered to plead guilty to lesser charge, court hears
item.description: Reinhardt Bosch has been charged with the murder and torture of his son Rhuan in 2022, and offered to plead guilty to the lesser charge of manslaughter, a court has heard.
item.dc:creator: Talissa Siganto

media:group.media:description: Reinhardt Bosch has been charged with torturing and killing his son Rhuan in 2022.

item.title: Coroner reopens Jayden Penno-Tompsett inquest after remains found
item.description: The New South Wales man's remains were found in the Queensland bush last October, years after an inquest determined his death was not suspicious.
item.dc:creator: Chloe Chomicki

media:group.media:description: Jayden Penno-Tompsett went missing on New Years Eve in 2017.

item.title: Lake Cargelligo gunman died 'very shortly after' triple murder
item.description: NSW Police focused the search for triple murderer Julian Ingram based on sightings by the public, but his body was found between the two major search areas.
item.dc:creator: Jostina Basta and Xanthe Gregory

media:group.media:description: Police have set up a crime scene at the location in the Round Hill National Park as they continue working to identify the body.

item.title: 'Terrible conversation': Smith opens up on call to sack childhood coach
item.description: Cameron Smith is leaving no stone unturned in his bid to correct his recent form slump, even if that means parting ways with someone he considers a friend.

media:group.media:description: Cameron Smith has turned to a new coach in a bid to arrest an alarming form slide.

item.title: Life sentence for driver in deadly car chase fuelled by jealous rage
item.description: The court in Toowoomba heard Clinton Gordon Sutcliffe was motivated by revenge after discovering his on-again off-again girlfriend had started a relationship with the 22-year-old victim.
item.dc:creator: Cindy Wockner and Grace Nakamura

media:group.media:description: Clinton Gordon Sutcliffe was found guilty of the murder of Dale Aaron McKenna.

item.title: Judge's social media posts at centre of sexual abuse verdict appeal
item.description: A former Hobart teacher sued by a former student over allegations of repeated sexual abuse has filed an appeal on the grounds of social media posts made by the judge presiding over his case.
item.dc:creator: Sophie Jaggers

media:group.media:description: Stephen Fane Noga, who was sued last month over allegations of repeated sexual abuse, has filed an appeal in the Hobart Supreme Court.

item.title: Cage fighter guilty of manslaughter after sparring session death of friend
item.description: Bradley Fletcher has been found not guilty of murder but guilty of manslaughter for killing his best friend during a backyard sparring session and leaving his body by a footpath.
item.dc:creator: Jamie McKinnell

media:group.media:description: Bradley Fletcher has been found guilty of manslaughter for killing Bradley Evennett after a backyard sparring session in March 2024.

item.title: Record payout after billionaire built mine without traditional owners' permission
item.description: Andrew Forrest's Fortescue has been ordered to pay $150 million to WA traditional owners after mining their land without permission.
item.dc:creator: Andrea Mayes

media:group.media:description: A composite image of Andrew Forrest (left) and Michael Woodley situated at a Fortescue mine in WA.

item.title: Qld government to continue with Paradise Dam rebuild despite advice
item.description: The board of Sunwater has written to the state government, labelling the rebuild of Paradise Dam "grossly disproportionate" and should be "no further considered".
item.dc:creator: Claudia Williams

media:group.media:description: The government says it will push on with the Paradise Dam rebuild, despite advice against the project from Sunwater.
